description_long: This is a modern, minimalist chaise with a rectangular seat and backrest upholstered in a dark fabric, likely charcoal or navy. The seat and backrest both feature a series of evenly spaced, light-colored buttons, giving the surface a subtle tufted appearance. The chaise is supported by four slender, straight metal legs with a black finish, providing a slight elevation above the ground. The backrest is of medium height and runs the full width of the seat, with no visible curvature or segmentation. There are no armrests, resulting in a clean, open silhouette. The overall design is sleek and functional, suitable for urban environments such as parks, lobbies, or public waiting areas.
description: A sleek, button-tufted chaise with dark upholstery, straight metal legs, and no armrests, featuring a minimalist and modern design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the seat and backrest, with visible button tufting and both front legs.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the chaise's depth, the thickness of the seat and backrest, and the absence of armrests.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the backrest and the two rear legs, with no decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the seat and backrest profile, button details, and leg positioning.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is a straight, rectangular panel of medium height, running the full width of the seat. It features a row of evenly spaced buttons for a tufted effect, with no visible curvature or segmentation.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side.
attribute_leg_style: The legs are straight, slender, and cylindrical, made of metal with a black finish. They are simple and functional, with no decorative elements.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: The upholstery is solid in color with a subtle button-tufted pattern, featuring evenly spaced light-colored buttons on both the seat and backrest.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: There are two main attached cushions: one forming the seat and one forming the backrest, both integrated into the frame with no loose or layered cushions visible.
